nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef slopes and rubble zones, typically in areas with moderate tidal flow; occurs from just below the surface to at least 15 m depth

decorPreferences: open placement with spacing from neighboring corals

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae symbiosis. Supplemental target feeding with micro-plankton, phytoplankton, or baby brine shrimp is generally considered beneficial but has not been reported as required for survival. Iodine, strontium, and trace element supplementation are commonly recommended.

goodWith: other soft corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: slow-growing stony corals without adequate spacing

summary: Paralemnalia Tree Coral is a finger-like, arborescent soft coral (family Lemnaliidae) with upright branching colonies and fine star-shaped polyps. Beginner-friendly and photosynthetic; tolerates a wide range of reef conditions.
careNotes:
Note: Paralemnalia is a soft coral genus commonly listed in the trade simply as 'Tree Coral' or 'Finger Leather Coral.' The scientificName is set to 'Paralemnalia sp.' to reflect the trade convention, as multiple species (most commonly P. thyrsoides) are sold under these names. The family classification has been updated to Lemnaliidae per WoRMS/OBIS; older sources list Nephtheidae.

Growth form and appearance: Paralemnalia forms an upright, branching colony of finger-like lobes arising from a fleshy, encrusting base. Colony color is typically beige to light tan-brown, with fine star-shaped polyps extending from branch tips when open. Wild specimens of P. thyrsoides tend to develop thickened, asparagus-like lobes from a basal encrusting mass.

Lighting: Generally advised to maintain low to moderate light — approximately 50–150 PAR. LiveAquaria lists this coral as 'moderate to high,' but cross-reference with related Nephtheidae genera (Capnella, Nephthea) and the species' relatively shallow (1–15 m) natural depth range suggests it is comfortable in moderate reef lighting. Acclimate new specimens gradually.

Flow: Moderate water movement is generally recommended. Sources variously describe requirements as 'medium to strong' (LiveAquaria) or 'moderate' (Reeflex). Gentle, alternating, non-laminar flow that allows polyp extension is preferred; strong direct laminar flow may cause persistent polyp retraction.

Placement: The coral can be placed across a wide range of rockwork positions. LiveAquaria recommends the middle of the aquarium; it has been observed thriving in lower to middle reef zones in the wild. Adequate clearance of 5–10 cm from neighboring corals is advisable due to potential allelopathy.

Allelopathy: LiveAquaria notes that Paralemnalia 'can produce toxins' and that 'adequate space needs to be provided between them and other corals.' Running activated carbon in the sump is commonly recommended in mixed-coral tanks, especially near sensitive SPS species.

Fragging: Paralemnalia has been reported to be very easy to propagate by cutting or detaching a branch and placing it on rubble or a frag plug. New growth tends to attach within a few days. Branches may also naturally detach in high-flow conditions.

Care level disagreement: LiveAquaria rates this coral as 'Moderate' care; Reeflex rates it 'Easy.' Given its tolerance of varied water parameters and forgiving temperament — consistent with other Nephtheidae family members — beginner has been adopted as the final rating. The LiveAquaria 'Moderate' rating may reflect its preference for supplemental trace elements rather than any intrinsic difficulty.

Size note: Reported adult sizes range from 10 cm (Reeflex sp.01) to 15 cm (P. thyrsoides, Reeflex) to up to 17.8 cm/7 inches (LiveAquaria). Final range of 5–15 cm reflects an average of confirmed sources.
breedingNotes: Sexual reproduction is not commonly practiced in the hobby. Paralemnalia propagates in captivity by vegetative fragmentation — cutting or detaching a branch and placing it on rubble or a frag plug is generally sufficient to establish a new colony. Frags tend to attach within a few days without adhesive.

primary.name: LiveAquaria – Assorted Tree Coral
primary.notes: Primary source; provided temperature, salinity, pH, alkalinity, care level, flow, placement, feeding, compatibility, and size data.

additional.name: Reeflex – Paralemnalia sp. 01
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (24–26°C), size (to 10 cm), care level (Easy), flow (moderate), lighting (moderate), minimum tank size (100 L). Temperature and size slightly narrower than LiveAquaria; averaged for final values.

additional.name: Reeflex – Paralemnalia thyrsoides
additional.notes: Cross-checked P. thyrsoides size (up to 15 cm / 5.91 in), temperature upper bound (up to 28°C), depth (6–15 m), and diet (zooxanthellae/light). Size and depth data incorporated.

additional.name: AquaMate Capnella Coral entry (Nephtheidae family reference)
additional.notes: Used as cross-reference for Nephtheidae family parameters (PAR 75–150, temp 23–28°C, SG 1.023–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, kH 8–12, calcium 380–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm). Paralemnalia and Capnella share family-level husbandry traits.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Paralemnalia (taxonomy)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Family: Nephtheidae [older classification], Order: Malacalcyonacea) and native range ('Africa, Malesia and Australia'). WoRMS/OBIS updated family to Lemnaliidae; both noted in careNotes.
